notes: Sanity-check of the parser path under a long single JSONL line. The driver provokes a ~5000-word single-sentence reply (~30 KB) — well under irrlicht's 64 MB tailer cap (#270) and even the old bufio.Scanner ~2 MB limit, so it parses normally rather than truly stressing the cap. Gemini's transport is FilesUnderRoot (~/.gemini/tmp/<project>/chats/*.jsonl) over the SHARED core/pkg/tailer (bufio.NewReaderSize + readLineCapped; lines >64 MB logged-and-skipped past, never stalled). The user-prompt line emits user_message → working; Gemini has no explicit end-of-turn marker, so the parser infers turn_done from the final text-only 'gemini' message with no open tool calls → working→ready. Spec asserts only the state arc: the absence of a parser stall is INFERRED from the arc completing — if the tailer wedged on the long line, working→ready would never fire and the phase contract would time out. turn_end anchors to turn_start so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the birth ready. observations.model ('gemini-3-flash-preview') is the live-verified default boot model and is PROVISIONAL — record settles it; a different live default is a string update, not a daemon bug.

text: Session appears in ready shortly after launch

Oversized assistant line is processed (well under the 64 MB cap); final text-only 'gemini' message with no tool calls → working → ready. Completion of this transition is the spec's parser-resilience proof.
